extraction.one_line_summary: GROENEWEGEN Dylan won Stage 3 bunch sprint with a perfectly timed bike throw over VAN AERT after getting caught in a crash at 10km to go.
tactical_patterns: GROENEWEGEN won despite crash involvement at 10km out; adrenaline spike from near-crashes can enhance sprint performance on recovery.
VAN AERT moved left over SAGAN in final meters possibly illegally, mirroring SAGAN's own relegation incident—no consistency from officials.
CORT Magnus attacked solo at race start for uncontested KOM point to avoid tie scenarios, prioritizing mathematical certainty over energy conservation.

rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: positive: first Tour stage win in nearly three years post-9-month ban, showing full sprint form recovery after controversial 2020 crash
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: negative: three consecutive second places despite superior positioning suggests either poor bike-throw timing or tactical hesitation in final 10 meters
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: neutral: got squeezed by VAN AERT but only finished fourth, indicating declining sprint power compared to positional disadvantage
rider_signals.JAKOBSEN Fabio: positive: fifth place after being on wrong side of SAGAN yesterday shows consistent positioning improvement stage-over-stage
